content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The changes are well-structured and achieve the goal of improving failure diagnostics. Error paths in process_backend.rs now consistently include stdout_len, stderr_len, and truncated tail content. The engine.rs runtime log now includes failure_class and error message. Claude CLI gets --verbose on retries. Tests cover the new diagnostic fields and --verbose injection. One minor bug found: duplicate --verbose flag on stale-session retries when attempt_number > 1.

## Amendments

1. In src/adapters/process_backend.rs around line 1493-1504, the stale-session retry path clones the original request (preserving attempt_number), calls build_command (which adds --verbose when attempt_number > 1), then unconditionally push_arg("--verbose") again. When attempt_number > 1, --verbose appears twice in the CLI args. Guard the push_arg with a check: only add --verbose if build_command hasn't already added it (e.g., `if fresh_request.attempt_number <= 1 { fresh_prepared.push_arg(...) }`).
   Rationale: While most CLIs silently ignore duplicate flags, this is an unintended double-insertion. If a future Claude CLI version treats duplicate --verbose differently (e.g., increases verbosity levels), the behavior would be surprising. The comment already explains the intent of not bumping attempt_number to avoid misrepresenting retry count, but doesn't account for build_command already inserting the flag.
